items.body: The John Deere Classic returns to TPC Deere Run in Silvis, Illinois, for its 55th edition as the Tour's last U.S. stop before the Open Championship. Ben Griffin and Chris Gotterup share betting favoritism in a 144-player field that leans birdie-friendly on a 7,327-yard par-71 layout, with Jackson Koivun making his professional debut and Brian Campbell defending after last year's breakthrough win. Jordan Spieth, a two-time champion here, headlines the course-history angle, while a redesigned fourth hole replaces the iconic Hewitt Tree with new bunkers and added length, and Saturday's third round lands on the Fourth of July as America marks its 250th birthday.

items.label: Ben Griffin (+1300 to +1400):
items.body: The co-favorite and a three-time winner in 2025 who finished T5 here in 2024, with recent form that includes a T10 at the Travelers and the approach game to attack TPC Deere Run's scoring opportunities.

items.label: Chris Gotterup (+1375 to +1400):
items.body: The other co-favorite and one of the highest-ranked players in the field, whose ball striking and rising profile make him a natural fit on a course that rewards precision wedges and hot putting.

items.label: Keith Mitchell (+1750 to +2200):
items.body: A proven winner whose steady tee-to-green game profiles well on a tree-lined Midwest layout that still asks for accuracy off the tee.

items.label: Keegan Bradley (+2500 to +2600):
items.body: A major champion and Ryder Cup stalwart who can capitalize when the putter heats up on a venue that traditionally produces low winning scores.

items.label: J.T. Poston (+2700 to +2800):
items.body: The 2022 champion at TPC Deere Run who knows how to navigate the soft, receptive conditions and birdie-heavy setup that define this week.

items.label: Jordan Spieth (+2800 to +3300):
items.body: A two-time winner in Silvis with deep Quad Cities history, returning to a venue where his creativity and iron play have produced playoff victories in the past.

items.label: Jackson Koivun (+2000 to +2800):
items.body: The reigning Haskins Award winner making his professional debut after a T23 at the U.S. Open and a T11 here as an amateur last year.

items.label: Michael Thorbjornsen (+3200):
items.body: A breakout candidate who finished runner-up to Davis Thompson in 2024 and profiles as a bomber who can take advantage of a getable par-71 track.

items.label: Rickie Fowler (+3600):
items.body: A popular draw in the field whose short-game touch and experience on scorable setups keep him in the conversation when the leaderboard goes low.

items.label: Davis Thompson (+4500 to +5100):
items.body: The 2024 champion who set the tournament record at 28-under and remains a live longshot if his putter finally cooperates on familiar Illinois turf.

items.label: Venue:
items.body: TPC Deere Run in Silvis, Illinois, in the Quad Cities region along the Illinois-Iowa border.

items.label: Established:
items.body: 1971; the 2026 edition is the 55th John Deere Classic on the PGA Tour.

items.label: Defending Champion:
items.body: Brian Campbell won in 2025 at 18-under, one year after Davis Thompson's record-setting 28-under victory.

items.label: Tradition:
items.body: A beloved Midwest summer stop tied to the John Deere brand, with strong local support, charity impact, and a reputation as one of the Tour's most fan-friendly events.

items.label: Course:
items.body: TPC Deere Run, Silvis, Illinois.

items.label: Dates:
items.body: July 2 through July 5, 2026.

items.label: Purse:
items.body: $8.8 million.

items.label: Format:
items.body: 144-player field, 72-hole stroke play.

items.label: Course Profile:
items.body: A 7,327-yard par-71 D.A. Weibring and Chris Gray design that plays soft and scorable in summer, rewarding precise wedge play and hot putters, with tree-lined fairways and a redesigned fourth hole that adds bunkering and length after the removal of the Hewitt Tree.

items.label: Coverage:
items.body: Thursday and Friday on Golf Channel (4-7 p.m. ET); weekend rounds on Golf Channel (1-3 p.m. ET) and CBS (3-6 p.m. ET Saturday and Sunday).

items.label: Event Window:
items.body: Competition runs July 2 through July 5, 2026.
